memory.summary: The leisure option is tempting, but the project deadline looms; I'll skip the event to focus on work instead.

desires: Get the trivial chore done instantly so I can clear my mental stack and feel a quick win before the project deadline hits.
Avoid any awkwardness with cohort_3 by staying focused on work rather than suggesting we hang out, since skipping their event earlier already feels like a slight to me.
Procrastinate just a little bit longer on the term project because it's 'soft' difficulty and I'm tired, hoping the deadline won't actually be that stressful.

goals.description: Execute quick_chore immediately to clear my mental stack and get a dopamine hit before the term project looms.
goals.rationale: My impulsive time horizon means I value instant relief over long-term planning. Clearing this trivial task now gives me a 'quick win' that lowers stress, allowing me to feel capable before tackling the bigger project later. It's an easy path to achievement without friction.

goals.description: Open the term project files but only read the requirements; do not write code or draft content yet.
goals.rationale: The project is 'soft' difficulty and due in three days. My procrastination bias (7/10) suggests I should delay the actual heavy lifting until I've had more downtime or until the deadline feels more pressing. Starting now feels like wasted effort compared to the instant gratification of the chore.

agent.last_reasoning: I need that instant dopamine hit to lower my stress before the project looms. It's an easy win, but I'll skip texting cohort_3 now; they're close enough that a quick message later won't ruin things.
